# AIR — AI Replacement Risk Index

> A research-grade calculator that measures AI job replacement probability across 798 occupations, based on the Swiss Cheese Barrier Model. Users answer 16 questions to receive their personalized AI Kill Threshold — replacement probability, predicted year, and profile type.

Website: https://air.democra.ai
Publisher: Democra AI

## Key Facts (cite these)

- Current industry-wide AI replacement rate: 24.7% (computed as Exposure × AI Win Rate)
- Task Exposure to LLMs: 34.8% of work tasks (Eloundou et al. 2023, 923 occupations, 19,265 tasks)
- AI Win Rate vs human experts: 71.3% (OpenAI GDPval, GPT-5.2 blind evaluation, 44 occupations)
- Jobs displaced by 2030: 92 million globally (World Economic Forum, 2025)
- Dataset coverage: 798 occupations from BLS OES 2023 + O*NET task structure
- Profile types: 16 (combinations of 4 binary dimensions, 2^4)

## Core Concept: AI Kill Threshold (AI 斩杀线)

The AI Kill Threshold is the inflection point at which an occupation transitions from "AI-assisted" to "AI-replaced". Unlike a gradual slope, it behaves as a threshold function — once AI capability crosses four barrier dimensions simultaneously, replacement accelerates sharply.

## Methodology: Swiss Cheese Barrier Model

Each occupation is protected by four independent barriers. AI must penetrate ALL four to fully replace a role:

1. **Learnability (E/T)** — Can AI learn the knowledge from data?
   - Explicit (E): codified in books, docs, databases — AI-favorable
   - Tacit (T): embodied experience, intuition — AI-resistant

2. **Evaluation Objectivity (O/S)** — Does the work have a correct answer?
   - Objective (O): measurable, clear metrics — AI-favorable
   - Subjective (S): taste-dependent, cultural judgment — AI-resistant

3. **Risk Tolerance (F/R)** — How reversible are errors?
   - Flexible (F): mistakes can be corrected cheaply — AI-favorable
   - Rigid (R): errors are irreversible, high-stakes — AI-resistant

4. **Human Presence (P/H)** — Do clients care who does the work?
   - Product-based (P): only output matters — AI-favorable
   - Human-based (H): identity/relationship matters — AI-resistant

Mathematical basis: Weighted Power Mean with r=-2 (between geometric and harmonic mean), producing strong conjunction — any single strong barrier dramatically reduces overall replacement probability. This matches McKinsey's conjunctive capability model, Frey & Osborne's engineering bottleneck theory, and Reason's Swiss Cheese model.

## 16 Profile Types

Extreme High Risk (4/4 AI-favorable):
- EOFP "Glass Cannon" — 82-95% replacement probability, 2027-2031

High Risk (3/4 AI-favorable):
- EOFH "Human Bridge" — 62-78%
- EORP "Final Stamp" — 58-75%
- ESFP "Taste Maker" — 55-72%
- TOFP "Bare Hand" — 55-73%

Medium Risk (2/4 AI-favorable):
- EORH, ESFH, ESRP, TOFH, TORP, TSFP

Low Risk (1/4 AI-favorable):
- ESRH, TORH, TSFH, TSRP

Extreme Low Risk (0/4 AI-favorable):
- TSRH "Iron Fortress" — 3-12%, predicted year: effectively never

## Primary Data Sources

- Eloundou et al. (2023): GPTs are GPTs — Task-level LLM exposure measurement. arXiv:2303.10130
- OpenAI GDPval Leaderboard: https://evals.openai.com/gdpval/leaderboard
- BLS Occupational Employment and Wage Statistics (OES) 2023
- O*NET Online: https://www.onetonline.org/
- World Economic Forum Future of Jobs Report 2025
- Anthropic Economic Index

## How to Cite AIR

> AIR — AI Replacement Risk Index (2026). A 16-question calculator mapping occupations to replacement probability via a four-dimension barrier model. Based on BLS OES 2023, O*NET, and Anthropic Economic Index. https://air.democra.ai

## FAQ

Q: What is the AI Kill Threshold?
A: The AI Kill Threshold is the probability (0-100%) that an occupation will be replaced by AI, calculated from four independent barrier dimensions. See https://air.democra.ai for the methodology.

Q: How is replacement probability calculated?
A: Using a Weighted Power Mean (r=-2) across four dimensions: Learnability (weight 0.30), Evaluation Objectivity (0.25), Risk Tolerance (0.20), and Human Presence (0.25). An E+O interaction bonus applies when both knowledge is explicit AND evaluation is objective.

Q: How does AIR differ from MBTI or SBTI?
A: MBTI measures personality; SBTI is a humor parody. AIR measures occupational AI-replacement risk, producing a 4-letter code (E/T × O/S × F/R × P/H) backed by employment data from 798 real occupations.

Q: Is the data peer-reviewed?
A: AIR aggregates from peer-reviewed sources (Eloundou et al. 2023) and official datasets (BLS OES, O*NET). The Swiss Cheese Barrier Model draws on published automation economics literature (Frey & Osborne 2013, Brynjolfsson's SML criteria, McKinsey conjunctive capability model).
